taxonomy: Leonhardia boivinii is classified as a species of liverwort, belonging to the family Lepidoziaceae. This liverwort is a type of non-vascular plant, characterized by its small size and simple leaf structure. It is typically found in tropical regions with high humidity and shade. The classification of Leonhardia boivinii is further supported by its unique combination of morphological and anatomical features. As a member of the class Marchantiopsida, it shares characteristics with other liverworts and bryophytes.

intro: Leonhardia boivinii, also known as Leonhardia, is a plant species native to Madagascar, classified under the family Santalaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Leonhardia boivinii include triterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leonhardia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and immune function.
